prompt:
The parts-catalog export keeps lying about what actually broke. Last night the transform choked on a bad record, but what hit the pager was `CleanupError: discard failed: staging volume offline` — the real data error was nowhere in the alert, we only found it by grepping __context__ in a debugger. Worse, after that crash the ops dashboard still showed the staging session as OPEN, so this morning's run refused to start until someone cleared it by hand.

Separately, when finalize hiccups on an otherwise clean run, the backend logs show us hitting it with a discard right after the failed finalize — two terminal calls on one session, which the staging service explicitly does not allow.

`python3 test_stagewriter.py` reproduces all of it with a scripted backend. Fix stagewriter.py so the caller always sees the original processing failure (cleanup trouble gets recorded on the session, not thrown over the top), the session ends up closed on every path, and each session settles with exactly one terminal backend call. Don't touch the tests.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0 on the buggy fixture; test_stagewriter.py exits 1: in test_cleanup_error_does_not_mask_primary the caller sees CleanupError from discard instead of the original ValueError (AssertionError: visible failure should be the processing error, got CleanupError), with the session still marked open; test_finalize_error_with_no_fallback_discard also fails because a failed finalize is followed by a second terminal discard call
